revision.text:
[[cs:Ikagašikome]][[en:Ikagashikome]][[fa:اکاگاشیکومه]][[ja:伊香色謎命]][[zh:伊香色谜命]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0097|2|17}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|1=Emperor Kaika}}; {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|children={{ill|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|qid=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Princess Mimatsuhime|qid=Q11489233|1=Princess Mimatsuhime}}; {{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}|father={{ill|Ōhesoki|qid=Q106106718|1=Ōhesoki}}}} {{Short description|Japanese Empress}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 152–97 BC | succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] | reign1 = appointed in 97 BC | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}} | issue = {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}, grandfather of {{Nihongo|[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]|武内宿禰}},
{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}},
{{Nihongo|Princess Mimatsuhime|御真津比売命}} | house = [[Mononobe clan]] | father = Ōhesoki }} '''Ikagashikome'''{{efn|There are two ways this name is transcribed: "Ika-gashiko-me" is used by ''Tsutomu Ujiya'', while "Ika-shiko-me" is used by [[William George Aston]].{{cite book|title=Nihon shoki|author=Ujiya, Tsutomu|year=1988|isbn=978-0-8021-5058-5|page=121}}}} was a concubine of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}},{{cite book |author=Aston, William George. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 2 |date=1896 |publisher=The Japan Society London |isbn=9780524053478 |page=109 & 149–150 |author-link=William George Aston}} and later {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}} and mother of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. She was the daughter of Ōhesoki. She was concubine of Emperor Kōgen, with whom she had one child: {{Nihongo|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|彦太忍信命}}, grandfather of {{Nihongo|[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]|武内宿禰}}.{{cite web |author=Shimazu Norifumi |date=March 15, 2006 |title=Takeshiuchi no Sukune |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=548 |access-date=May 16, 2019 |work=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}} Later, she married Emperor Kaika and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} and {{Nihongo|Princess Mimatsuhime|御真津比売命}}. She was the empress dowager from 97 BC.Anston, p. 150 (Vol. 1) ==Family tree== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Notelist== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | years = 152–97 BC | before = {{ill|Utsushikome|ja|欝色謎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}} | after = {{ill|Mimaki-hime|en|Mimaki-hime|qid=Q11071228|lt=Mimaki-hime|1=Mimaki-hime}} }} {{succession box | title = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] | years = appointed in 97 BC | before = Utsushikome | after = Mimaki-hime }} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Isonokami Faith}} [[en:Ikagashikome]] [[cs:Ikagašikome]] [[fa:اکاگاشیکومه]] [[ja:伊香色謎命]] [[zh:伊香色谜命]] {{wikidata link|Q10885176}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese people]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
